sections.paragraph:
Original: כּיּוּן

Transliteration: kı̂yûn

Phonetic: kee-yoon'

BDB Definition:

Chiun = "an image" or " pillar"
  1. probably a statue of the Assyrian-Babylonian god of the planet Saturn and used to symbolise Israelite apostasy

Origin: from H3559

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name
